name: Ocana - circular tour of the historic center
name_original: Ocaña – tour circular por casco histórico
region_intro: Hiking around Ocaña offers diverse natural landscapes characterized by unique geological formations and lush scenery. The region features prominent hills that provide panoramic vistas of the surrounding area. Hikers can explore striking rock formations and agricultural landscapes, including coffee farms, that define the terrain. Best hiking trails around Ocaña The most popular hiking route is Sources of Cabañas de Yepes - circular route (PR-TO 16), a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the natural surroundings of Cabañas de Yepes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ocana - circular tour of the historic center, an easy 3.1 miles (4.9 km) path. This route offers a walk through the historic center of Ocaña. Local hikers also love the Plaza de Gutiérrez Cárdenas loop from Ocaña, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through the local landscape around Ocaña, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Ocaña is defined by diverse natural landscapes, unique geological formations, and prominent hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Ocaña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 290 hikers have used komoot to explore Ocaña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Ocaña region?
A: The Ocaña region offers a diverse network of over 60 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 27 easy trails, 38 moderate options, and 1 challenging route for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Ocaña?
A: Ocaña is characterized by its diverse and striking natural landscapes. Hikers can explore unique geological formations, lush scenery, and prominent hills offering panoramic vistas. A major highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardín del Príncipe&lt;/a&gt;, known for its distinctive rock formations, and various hidden waterfalls nestled within the green surroundings.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ocaña?
A: Yes, Ocaña offers several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018401538/sources-of-cabanas-de-yepes-circular-route-pr-to-16" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sources of Cabañas de Yepes - circular route (PR-TO 16)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2-mile (5.2 km) trail that explores the natural surroundings and takes about 1 hour 25 minutes to complete, making it a great choice for a family outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Ocaña?
A: Absolutely. Many hikers prefer circular routes for convenience. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17316184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Gutiérrez Cárdenas loop from Ocaña&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.3-mile (8.5 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 10 minutes to complete, offering a scenic journey through the local landscape.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Ocaña's surroundings feature several interesting landmarks. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt; or admire the intricate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6135943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Hercules and Antaeus&lt;/a&gt;. For a different experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021050945/ocana-circular-tour-of-the-historic-center" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ocana - circular tour of the historic center&lt;/a&gt; allows you to explore the town's heritage directly.

Q: Are there any trails that offer scenic viewpoints or panoramic views?
A: Yes, the region is known for its prominent hills that provide stunning panoramic views. Cerro del Tabor, for instance, offers an invigorating trek that rewards hikers with breathtaking vistas of Ocaña and the surrounding mountains, ideal for photography.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Ocaña?
A: The trails in Ocaña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from unique geological formations to lush landscapes, and the variety of routes available for all ability levels.

Q: Are there any opportunities to see wildlife while hiking in Ocaña?
A: While specific wildlife sightings can vary, the lush landscapes and river areas, such as the Algodonal River, provide habitats for colorful birds and other local wildlife. Exploring quieter trails, especially during dawn or dusk, increases your chances of spotting native species.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like lakes or springs?
A: Yes, the Ocaña area includes several natural water features. Hikers can discover highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Seca de las Esteras&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/407021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinescos Pond&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7003981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacabras Spring&lt;/a&gt;, offering tranquil spots along the routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ocaña?
A: While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, regions with diverse natural landscapes like Ocaña often offer pleasant hiking conditions in spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring cooler weather, so checking local forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there any challenging hiking trails for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous adventure, Ocaña does offer at least one difficult-grade trail. These routes typically feature greater elevation changes or more rugged terrain, providing a rewarding challenge for experienced hikers.
tips: La Fuente Grande is an example of Herrerian-style Renaissance architecture. Its construction began in 1573 and was completed in 1578. Its project is attributed to Juan de Herrera, although the material realization is due to the local master masons Blas Hernández and Francisco Sánchez, and the stonemasons Lucas and Pedro de Villa.

The origins of the Church of Santa María de Ocaña date back to the 12th century. It was the first church open to Christian worship in the town. The temple was built on an old mosque.
The church has a Latin cross plan with a single nave with three sections. It has 5 chapels and a baptistery-chapel. Noteworthy is the majestic choir at the foot of the temple.
Nice square next to the Church of Santa María de la Asunción.

The Plaza Mayor de Ocaña is a baroque square from the 18th century.
It is made up of 18 arches on the larger sides and 17 on the smaller sides. Definitely worth knowing.

It is a Renaissance construction from the 16th century. The church has a 3-nave floor plan with a polygonal apse. The paintings in the altarpiece are by the Dominican Father Julio Ibañez.
The importance of the choir stalls must be highlighted.
Declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in the year 2000.

The Palace of the Cárdenas, also known as the Dukes of Frías, next to the Plaza Mayor and the Fuente Grande, are the declared monuments of historical interest in the town of Ocaña.
It was built in the 16th century and its traces show transitional elements from the Gothic to the Renaissance.

The Rollo de Justicia or Picota is a stone column, topped by a wrought iron cross, located in the Plaza de José María Prada, in front of the Lope de Vega Theater.
It is a bundle of eight limestone pillars. In the central part of the shafts, in the same way as in the capitals, there is a voluminous collar ornamented with pearls.
On a historic building from the 16th century, the current theater is a main cultural enclave of the Villa, where important events of a cultural and recreational nature take place.
Bust of Isabel la Católica in Ocaña, place of refuge for the Queen, exiled from the Court of Enrique IV.

The Tower of the Church of San Martín belonged to the disappeared church of the same name; It is in the Renaissance-Herrerian style and its construction began in 1562 and was completed in 1577. The works were directed by D. Francisco Sánchez and it was carried out by the brothers Lucas and Pedro de la Villa.
